processors.name: WiFi State
processors.description: Tracks WiFi connection state throughout the log: enabled/disabled, SSID, signal strength, and IP address. Supports AOSP (WifiStateMachine) and Samsung (SemWifiApAdvancedAutohotspotService) devices.

processors.name: Battery State
processors.description: Tracks battery level, charging status, and plug source from BatteryService. Supports Samsung and AOSP formats.

processors.name: App Lifecycle
processors.description: Tracks foreground app changes, activity launches, and crashes via ActivityManager.

processors.name: Network Connectivity
processors.description: Tracks network connectivity state: connected/disconnected, network type (WIFI, MOBILE, etc.), and validation status.

processors.name: Auto Blocker
processors.description: Tracks Samsung Auto Blocker state. When enabled, blocks USB debugging, ADB commands, app sideloading, and developer certificates.

processors.name: Connectivity Check Probes
processors.description: Captures every Android NetworkMonitor probe (DNS, HTTP, HTTPS, FALLBACK) with timing, result, and failure details. Highlights DNS resolution failures as a root cause of connectivity check failures.

processors.name: Connectivity Check State
processors.description: Tracks Android NetworkMonitor validation lifecycle per network. Monitors probe-level DNS failures, HTTP/HTTPS probe outcomes, and the final isCaptivePortal verdict.

processors.name: EBADF Error Tracker
processors.description: Finds EBADF (bad file descriptor) errors across all processes. Groups by PID and operation type. High EBADF rates often indicate FD recycling caused by an FD leak in another process.

processors.name: Exception Storm Detector
processors.description: Detects exception storms — bursts of 20+ identical errors within a 2-second window. Uses the BurstDetector aggregate to fire exactly once per rising edge.

processors.name: FD Exhaustion → EBADF Correlator
processors.description: Detects when a system_server FD spike (>900 FDs per Watchdog telemetry) precedes EBADF errors in other processes — indicating FD recycling as the root cause.

processors.name: GC Pressure Monitor
processors.description: Tracks ART GC events. Monitors heap used/max and GC duration. Classifies heap pressure as normal (<70%), stressed (70-85%), or critical (>85%).

processors.name: Process Kill Storm
processors.description: Captures ActivityManager process kills. Tracks kill rate, RSS freed per kill, and groups kills by reason. Useful for diagnosing memory pressure kill storms.

processors.name: System Server FD Monitor
processors.description: Extracts Samsung Watchdog FD telemetry from system_server. Tracks FD count over time, classifies into normal/elevated/critical states, and detects the boot FD spike pattern.

processors.name: System Server Heap Monitor
processors.description: Monitors Android system_server Java heap pressure via Watchdog !@Sync messages. Tracks heap used/max and pressure level over time.

processors.name: WLAN Disconnect Events
processors.description: Captures WiFi disconnection events from bugreport WifiStateMachine dumps and live logcat. Extracts IEEE 802.11 reason codes, locally-generated flags, BSSID, and SSID. Content-based dedup avoids bugreport cross-section duplication.

processors.name: Exception Stack Tracker
processors.description: Tracks exception stack traces across all tags — both fatal crashes (AndroidRuntime FATAL EXCEPTION) and non-fatal logged exceptions (Binder, system_server, etc.). Separates fatal vs non-fatal counts, shows which apps crashed and why, and groups non-fatal exceptions by source and type. System.err exceptions are attributed to the owning app package via stack frame analysis.

processors.name: WLAN Disconnect Tracker
processors.description: Tracks WiFi connection state with focus on disconnection events. Captures IEEE 802.11 reason codes, locally-generated flags, and BSSID from wpa_supplicant CTRL-EVENT messages. Uses DUMPSYS NORMAL section filtering to avoid bugreport cross-section duplication.

processors.name: DNS Server Stats
processors.description: Parses the Android dnsresolver service dump to extract per-server DNS statistics: query totals, success/error/timeout counts, RTT averages per protocol (UDP, DoH, DoT/TLS, TCP), rcode distributions, server reliability scores, and Private DNS mode.

processors.name: DNS Private Probe Tracker
processors.description: Parses PrivateDnsLog from the dnsresolver dump to build a timeline of DoT/DoH connection probe events. Computes per-probe latency, tracks repeated server failures, and surfaces the pattern of local DHCP servers that fail DoT while public resolvers succeed.

processors.name: DNS Query Events
processors.description: Tracks per-query DNS resolution events from NetdEventListenerService logcat. Shows requesting app, network ID, result (SUCCESS/FAIL/SKIPPED), and latency. Aggregates total/answered/failed/skipped counts with per-app and per-network breakdowns.

processors.name: Cellular Registration State
processors.description: Tracks cellular voice and data registration state, RAT (NR_SA, LTE, etc.), and operator name. Reconstructs the cellular registration timeline automatically from PhoneStateTracker and VolteServiceModule logs.

processors.name: Cellular Data Failure Reporter
processors.description: Surfaces why mobile data setup fails on Android. Captures trySetupData failures, data-allowed gate rejections, and no-APN conditions from DCT-C-0 and DataEnabledSettings tags. Extracts all data-enabled flag snapshots so the root cause (e.g. isUserDataEnabled=false) is immediately visible.

processors.name: Radio Data Call Reporter
processors.description: Tracks RIL-level data call setup and teardown from RILJ, DSM-C-0, DCT-C-0, and SST. Captures SETUP_DATA_CALL and DEACTIVATE_DATA_CALL commands, DATA_REGISTRATION_STATE polls, and pollStateDone transitions.

processors.name: Radio / Airplane Mode State
processors.description: Tracks airplane mode, data connection state, emergency-only mode, and SIM card state from MobileSignalController. Compound transitions capture all four fields atomically when possible.

processors.name: IMS / VoLTE Bearer Reporter
processors.description: Tracks IMS bearer setup and teardown events from logcat and bugreport. Extracts state transitions (CONNECTING → CONNECTED → DISCONNECTED), transport type (WWAN/WLAN), network type (LTE, NR), and interface name. Distinguishes 'IMS connected but no internet' situations from full connectivity loss.
